Entire plan — no user-facing interaction surface introduced
References section: 'ADR-0039 (A-4c)' and new anchoring + synonym rules introduced in AC1/AC2
findings.finding: The plan introduces two substantive new matching rules — legend-OR-option-domain anchoring (AC1) and polarity rules that ignore a shared head token (AC2). These represent architectural decisions on top of ADR-0039. It is unclear whether the plan intends to update ADR-0039 in place, append a new ADR (e.g. ADR-0040), or whether a brief changelog note within the existing ADR is sufficient. The plan text does not explicitly address documentation of the new rules. If the ADR is not updated, future maintainers will find the documented matcher behavior diverges from what is actually implemented.
findings.suggested_edit: Add an explicit doc step: 'Update ADR-0039 (or create ADR-0040) to record (a) the legend-OR-option-domain anchoring heuristic and (b) the shared-head-token polarity exclusion rule, with rationale referencing the mixed-status real-snap failure mode.'

findings.location: Entire plan — purely algorithmic/matching logic with no shared/global state introduced
Plan description and all Acceptance Criteria
AC-1: 're-recorded without changing the frozen determinism baseline (or with the baseline deliberately re-frozen + documented)'
findings.finding: AC-1 contains a conditional ('or with the baseline deliberately re-frozen + documented') that makes the observable outcome partially open-ended: an agent could satisfy it either by re-recording AND preserving the baseline OR by re-recording AND re-freezing, with no clear criterion for which branch was achieved. The primary outcome (coverage-map steps carry `advanced`/`path`) is observable, but the branch condition leaves the verification state ambiguous. The remaining ACs (unit tests pinning the retry/re-drive behaviour in AC-2 and AC-3, coupling-lint PASS in AC-4) are measurable and in-session completable. AC-4 ('HERMETIC/PORTABLE/REPEATABLE preserved; coupling-lint PASS') references coupling-lint as a concrete command, which is good, but 'HERMETIC/PORTABLE/REPEATABLE preserved' lacks a concrete proving command of its own.
findings.suggested_edit: Collapse the branch in AC-1 to a single durable outcome: 'Coverage-map trace steps carry a per-step `advanced` (and/or `path`) signal; the determinism baseline is re-frozen and the re-freeze is documented in the ADR/changelog.' Remove the ambiguous 'or'. For AC-4, replace 'HERMETIC/PORTABLE/REPEATABLE preserved' with the specific lint/test command that proves it.

findings.location: AC-1: 'advanced (and/or path)'; SCOPE item 1: 'persisted per step, which requires RE-RECORDING the coverage map (an LLM-driven exploration) and re-freezing the determinism baseline'
findings.finding: Two blocking ambiguities exist: (1) 'advanced (and/or path)' is unresolved — the plan does not decide which field(s) must be added; an executor must choose. This matters because the re-drive gating logic in AC-2 depends on exactly which field is gated on ('PORTABLE, no prototype literal'). (2) The re-recording step is described as 'an LLM-driven exploration' but the plan gives no instructions on HOW the agent should trigger, configure, or bound that exploration — no entry-point script, no max-steps, no acceptance threshold. An agent cannot proceed to re-record without making undocumented design choices. Additionally, 'bounded' in AC-2 ('re-drives a single non-advancing step bounded') does not state the retry count/limit.
findings.suggested_edit: Decide: use `advanced` (boolean) only, or both `advanced` and `path`. Document the exact re-recording command/script and its parameters. Specify the retry bound for the re-drive (e.g. 'up to N=2 re-drive attempts per step').

findings.location: AC-1, AC-2, AC-3, AC-4
findings.finding: No concrete proving command is given for any criterion: (a) AC-1 — no command to assert that re-recorded trace steps contain `advanced`/`path` (e.g. `jq` against the coverage-map file, or a snapshot test). (b) AC-2 names a 'new navigate-level unit test' but no test file path, test runner invocation, or expected output is specified; 'pinned by a new unit test' is a deliverable statement, not a proving command. (c) AC-3 similarly says 'pinned by a test' with no test runner command or file location. (d) AC-4 says 'coupling-lint PASS' — the only concrete command — but omits what command proves HERMETIC/PORTABLE/REPEATABLE. The plan also contains hedges: 'still returns a LOUD drifted/non-arrival' and 'genuinely unreachable surface still WARNs/STOPs loudly' rely on behavioural assertions with no automated check.
findings.suggested_edit: For each AC add a proving command, e.g.: AC-1: `node scripts/validate-coverage-map.mjs --require-field advanced`; AC-2: `npx jest navigate.test.mjs --testNamePattern 'reachByTrace re-drive'`; AC-3: `npx jest gather.test.mjs --testNamePattern 'arrival-retry'`; AC-4: `npm run lint:coupling`. Replace 'WARNs/STOPs loudly' with an assertion checked inside the named test.

findings.location: SCOPE item 1 vs AC-1 and AC-2; AC-4
findings.finding: Two coverage gaps: (1) The SCOPE body explicitly describes the re-recording/re-freezing of the determinism baseline as a deliverable ('requires RE-RECORDING the coverage map … and re-freezing the determinism baseline'). AC-1 only conditionally covers this ('or with the baseline deliberately re-frozen + documented'), leaving open the scenario where the baseline is simply not re-frozen at all — no criterion enforces the re-freeze as an unconditional outcome. (2) AC-4 ('HERMETIC/PORTABLE/REPEATABLE preserved; coupling-lint PASS') bundles two logically distinct requirements (portability and coupling-lint) into one criterion without a covering step or proof for the portability half, making part of it an orphan claim. No criterion explicitly covers the 'PORTABLE — gate on the trace's own advanced flag, never a label/prototype literal' requirement stated in Scope item 1; AC-2 mentions it parenthetically but does not make it a first-class verifiable outcome.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a standalone criterion: 'The determinism baseline is re-frozen; the re-freeze commit is referenced in the ADR.' Promote the portability requirement into a dedicated, independently verifiable criterion (e.g. 'No step in navigate.mjs references a label/prototype literal to gate re-drive; verified by code-search/lint rule').

findings.location: CONTEXT: 'RESOLVED for the conformance gate by a depth-aware fresh-context arrival-retry bound' vs SCOPE item 2: 'gather.mjs arrival-retry … Mirror the conformance depth-aware arrival-retry (arrivalRetryFor) into gather's reach'
findings.finding: The context section states that the run-0041 deep-reach arrival flake was 'RESOLVED' at the whole-surface level by ADR-0057, yet SCOPE item 2 adds a fresh-context arrival-retry to gather.mjs using the same mechanism — implying the problem is NOT fully resolved and gather is 'MORE fragile than conformance'. This is not a contradiction per se, but there is a genuine cross-section tension: the CONTEXT claims the root cause in Scope item 1 (silent no-op swallow in reachByTrace) is still open, while simultaneously saying it is 'RESOLVED' in the conformance gate. An agent reading only the CONTEXT would not proceed with Scope item 1 (since it looks fixed), but the AC mandates it. The plan never explicitly reconciles 'RESOLVED for conformance' with 'still needs fixing in reachByTrace/gather'.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a sentence to the CONTEXT clarifying: 'RESOLVED means the conformance gate no longer flakes; the underlying reachByTrace swallow and gather fragility are still present and tracked here.'

findings.location: Title, SCOPE, Acceptance Criteria
SCOPE sections 1 and 2; Acceptance Criteria (4 ACs)
findings.finding: The ticket joins two structurally independent goals with an explicit 'and': (1) persist a new per-step `advanced`/`path` field in the coverage-map + re-record it (a data-layer change requiring LLM-driven re-exploration and baseline re-freeze), and (2) mirror fresh-context arrival-retry into gather.mjs (a control-flow change in a separate module). These are independent along every axis: different files (navigate.mjs + coverage-map store vs gather.mjs), different layers (persistence/trace schema vs orchestration/retry logic), different riskiest unknowns (determinism-baseline stability vs gather retry semantics). There is no thin vertical slice / evidence-gated sequencing — both pieces are stated as co-equal parallel work. The plan acknowledges the deferral ('intentionally deferred') but doesn't structure them as child tickets or order them so the riskier piece (re-recording the coverage map) is gated on passing evidence before the second piece begins. Sizing signals: >3 files touched, >=3 layers (trace schema, navigate step-execution, gather orchestration), a new interface (per-step `advanced` field contract), and low scope-certainty on the re-recording step. The 4 ACs map 1:1 to the two goals plus two cross-cutting quality ACs, reinforcing the structural 'and'. Recommended fix: split into two child tickets — Ticket A: persist `advanced` in trace + re-record + reachByTrace step-level re-drive (unit-tested); Ticket B (gated on Ticket A merge + baseline stability): gather.mjs arrival-retry mirror. This gives a vertical slice ordering where the riskier LLM re-exploration is fully validated before gather.mjs is touched.
findings.suggested_edit:
Split into:
- Child A: 'Persist per-step `advanced` flag in coverage-map trace + reachByTrace step-level non-advance re-drive' (ACs 1, 2, 4)
- Child B (depends on A): 'Mirror depth-aware arrival-retry into gather.mjs' (ACs 3, 4)
Sequence B behind A so the new trace schema is stable before gather.mjs consumes it.

findings.location: AC-2: 'Pinned by a new navigate-level unit test (fake page): silent-no-op heals via single-step re-drive; repeated-name swallow prevented; genuine non-advance STILL STOPs.' AC-3: 'Pinned by a test.'
SCOPE section 1: 're-drives a single non-advancing step in-context (cheaper than abandoning ~50 steps)'; AC-2: 'bounded'
findings.finding: The plan introduces new I/O-adjacent behavior: a per-step re-drive retry loop inside reachByTrace and a depth-aware arrival-retry loop inside gather.mjs. The plan asserts the re-drive is 'cheaper than abandoning ~50 steps' and conformance retry is 'bounded' (>=6 attempts), but no explicit retry-count cap or latency ceiling is stated for either the step-level re-drive in reachByTrace or the gather.mjs arrival-retry. For the gather mirror specifically, the plan says 'same depth-aware fresh-context arrival-retry as conformance' — the existing arrivalRetryFor bound is referenced but not quoted, so its ceiling is only knowable from the live codebase. If that bound is reasonable and already documented in ADR-0057, this is fine; if not, unbounded retries on a failure path could significantly inflate wall-clock gather time. Cannot determine from plan text alone whether the existing conformance retry bound is an adequate cap for gather's deeper surfaces.
findings.suggested_edit: Explicitly state the retry-count cap for the step-level re-drive (e.g., 'max 1 re-drive per step') and reference or quote the arrivalRetryFor ceiling from ADR-0057 so the bound is visible in this ticket.

findings.location: AC-2: 'a permanently-stuck step still returns a LOUD drifted/non-arrival'; AC-3: 'a genuinely unreachable surface still WARNs/STOPs loudly'
findings.finding: The plan explicitly preserves the loud-STOP guarantee for both the reachByTrace re-drive (permanently-stuck step drifts, not silently swallowed) and the gather arrival-retry (unreachable surface WARNs/STOPs). Error states are surfaced, not swallowed. The re-drive is single-attempt ('single non-advancing step'), which is idempotent in effect. No new write operations or external state is introduced beyond the coverage-map re-recording, which is an LLM-driven exploration already handled by the existing baseline-freeze process.

findings.location: Entire plan
findings.finding: The plan introduces no new endpoints, data storage, PII handling, auth boundaries, credentials, roles, or secrets. It is purely internal navigation/orchestration logic and coverage-map trace schema changes. Security overlay is not applicable.

findings.location: AC-1: 're-recorded without changing the frozen determinism baseline (or with the baseline deliberately re-frozen + documented)'; SCOPE section 1: 'PORTABLE (gate on the trace's own advanced flag, never a label/prototype literal)'
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new per-step `advanced` (and/or `path`) field as a contract in the coverage-map trace schema. This is a cross-component interface change: navigate.mjs writes it, reachByTrace reads it, and gather.mjs implicitly depends on the same trace. The portability constraint ('gate on the trace's own advanced flag, never a label/prototype literal') is stated, which is good. However, the plan does not specify whether any downstream consumers of the coverage-map trace (e.g., reporting, replay tooling, other callers) need schema-migration handling, nor does it capture the schema change as an ADR update (ADR-0057 is referenced as context but no update or new ADR is called out). The re-freeze-or-not decision for the determinism baseline is flagged as a fork ('or with the baseline deliberately re-frozen + documented') without a decision criterion — this is an architectural choice that warrants documentation. Cannot determine from plan text alone whether other consumers exist.
findings.suggested_edit: Add to AC-1: 'The schema change (new `advanced`/`path` field) is documented as an amendment to ADR-0057 or a new ADR, with a decision on whether the baseline is re-frozen and why.' Also enumerate any other known consumers of the coverage-map trace to confirm no silent schema breakage.

findings.location: AC-1: 'Coverage-map trace steps carry a per-step `advanced` (and/or `path`) signal, re-recorded without changing the frozen determinism baseline (or with the baseline deliberately re-frozen + documented).'
findings.finding: The plan asserts two high-uncertainty outcomes without any empirical validation step: (1) that re-recording the coverage map will NOT change the frozen determinism baseline, and (2) that the single-step re-drive heuristic will reliably heal the ~17-identical-step swallow without introducing new loops or over-retries. Both are correctness claims that cannot be settled by reasoning alone — the first depends on LLM exploration reproducibility, and the second depends on real page-state behavior across stochastic sessions. No spike, RED fixture, or pilot with success metrics is planned before full build-out. The unit test mentioned in AC-2 tests the fake-page happy path but does not constitute an empirical probe against the actual stochastic environment.
findings.suggested_edit: Add an explicit pre-implementation spike: (a) re-record the coverage map once in a throwaway branch and diff the determinism baseline to confirm it is stable; (b) inject a synthetic single-step no-op into a test run and verify the re-drive heals it without looping. Gate the full build on both passing. Document the spike results in the ADR before freezing.

findings.location: AC-1: 're-recorded without changing the frozen determinism baseline (or with the baseline deliberately re-frozen + documented)'; SCOPE section: 'requires RE-RECORDING the coverage map'
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges that re-recording the coverage map is a schema-changing, potentially irreversible operation (the frozen determinism baseline may need to be re-frozen). However, it provides no rollback/back-out path: if the re-recorded map drifts from the previous baseline or introduces regressions, there is no described way to revert to the prior frozen state. The plan mentions 'or with the baseline deliberately re-frozen + documented' as an alternative but does not specify how the old baseline is preserved, how consumers of the coverage map (conformance gate, gather) tolerate the schema change during the transition, or what the expand-contract sequence looks like. Acknowledgement of the breaking change is present; a reversibility mechanism is absent.
findings.suggested_edit: Before re-recording, snapshot and tag the current frozen coverage map and determinism baseline in source control. Define an explicit rollback step: 'if post-recording diff shows unexpected surface changes, restore tagged snapshot and file a follow-on'. Describe the expand-contract sequence: old `{role,name,nth,kind,value}` steps without `advanced` should be treated as `advanced: true` by consumers until the new map is fully frozen, so existing conformance and gather runs are not broken mid-migration.

findings.location: Entire plan
Entire plan
CONTEXT: 'Follow-on to ADR-0057'; SCOPE sections 1 and 2; AC-4: 'HERMETIC/PORTABLE/REPEATABLE preserved; coupling-lint PASS'
findings.finding: The plan introduces two new architectural patterns — (1) a per-step `advanced` flag in the coverage-map schema, and (2) a depth-aware arrival-retry contract shared between conformance and gather — both of which constitute new contracts/interfaces. ADR-0057 is referenced but no new ADR or doc update is planned for these additions. If the frozen determinism baseline is re-frozen, ADR-0057 becomes stale (it documents the previous resolution). The plan does not specify whether a new ADR will be written or whether ADR-0057 will be amended, leaving future readers with a stale reference.
findings.suggested_edit: Either (a) create a new ADR (e.g. ADR-0058) documenting the per-step `advanced` schema change, the re-recording rationale, the step-level re-drive contract, and the shared arrivalRetryFor pattern; or (b) append a clearly dated amendment to ADR-0057. Add a docs checklist item to AC-4 explicitly requiring the ADR to be filed before merge.

findings.location: AC-1: 'Coverage-map trace steps carry a per-step `advanced` (and/or `path`) signal, re-recorded'; SCOPE section 1: 'requires RE-RECORDING the coverage map (an LLM-driven exploration) and re-freezing the determinism baseline'
findings.finding: The coverage map is shared/global state consumed by at least conformance (gate) and gather.mjs. The plan does not define: (a) WHO owns the re-recording trigger and when it fires (LLM exploration is non-deterministic — is re-recording a manual one-off, a CI step, or triggered on demand?); (b) UPDATE concurrency — if two agents or CI runs attempt re-recording simultaneously, is there a lock or CAS guard?; (c) how consumers (conformance gate, gather) behave when they read a partially-written or mid-migration coverage map (absence/staleness tolerance); (d) a RETIRE path for the old coverage-map format (does the old file accumulate alongside the new one, or is it replaced atomically?). The concurrency-safety of the LLM-driven re-recording write is entirely unspecified.
findings.suggested_edit: Add to the plan: (1) ownership — 'Re-recording is a manual, one-shot developer action, committed as a single atomic file replacement'; (2) concurrency guard — 'The coverage-map file is written atomically (write-to-temp then rename); CI is serialized on this file via a lock or branch protection'; (3) consumer tolerance — 'Steps missing `advanced` default to `advanced: true` in both navigate.mjs and gather.mjs until the new map is fully deployed'; (4) retirement — 'The old frozen baseline file is deleted in the same commit that introduces the new one, with a git-tag snapshot for rollback'.

findings.location: Plan context / scope sections
All five ACs
AC1: 'anchoring on the legend OR on option-domain coverage'; AC2: 'robust value-synonyms / a polarity rule that ignores a shared head token'
findings.finding:
Two design choices are left to the executor without specifying which path to take:
1. AC1 offers two distinct algorithmic strategies ('anchor on the legend' vs 'option-domain coverage') with no decision rule for which to prefer or how to combine them. An agent must invent the selection logic.
2. AC2 describes the desired outcome ('ignores a shared head token') but does not specify the rule: is it a stop-word list, a TF-IDF weight, a per-group frequency filter, or something else? 'Robust value-synonyms' is similarly under-specified — no synonym table, no expansion strategy, no collision threshold is stated.
These are real design decisions that block planning and will produce materially different implementations depending on which path the agent chooses.
findings.suggested_edit: Specify the chosen strategy for each: e.g. 'Use option-domain coverage as the primary anchor (≥N of Q question-type values present in group options); legend anchor is a fallback.' For the synonym/polarity rule: 'Strip any token that appears in >50% of option labels before scoring polarity; treat the remaining tokens as the distinguishing vocabulary.'

findings.location: AC1, AC2, AC3, AC4, AC5 — no 'steps' section anywhere in the plan
findings.finding:
The plan has zero implementation steps; it is entirely problem-description + ACs. Because there are no described steps:
(a) No proving command is supplied for any AC. 'Mixed-status resolves' and 'A-4c RED is flipped to GREEN' imply running scenario-match-test.mjs, but the command is never stated.
(b) AC2 ('4 abstract immigration values bind … distinctly') has no test assertion described that would confirm distinct binding vs. a tie.
(c) AC4 ('no prototype literal leaks (lint ENGINE_FILES)') names a lint command but does not specify it — the agent must guess the exact invocation.
(d) AC5 ('Closes A-4 AC2 for all three scenarios') references an external acceptance criterion that is not reproduced or linked with enough specificity to verify in-session.
Without steps, there is no union-of-steps to check against the end-state, satisfying none of the E6 binary sub-checks.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a numbered implementation-steps section and for each AC name the concrete command/assertion: e.g. 'Run `node scenario-match-test.mjs` and confirm the previously-RED mixed-status test now shows GREEN'; 'Run `eslint --rule ENGINE_FILES` and confirm zero prototype-literal violations'; 'Assert pickBest returns a unique winner (score delta > threshold) for each of the 4 immigration values against the real snapshot options.'

findings.location: AC5 vs AC3; 'What' section causal analysis vs AC1/AC2
findings.finding: AC3 ('mixed-status resolves … A-4c documented RED flipped to GREEN') and AC5 ('Closes A-4 AC2 for all three scenarios on real snap data') are partially overlapping: AC3 covers mixed-status specifically; AC5 covers all three scenarios including mixed-status. There is near-duplication of the mixed-status coverage claim. Additionally, the 'What' section identifies two root-cause fixes (legend anchoring + verbose-option polarity) both of which are covered by AC1 and AC2 respectively — bidirectional coverage holds. The near-duplication between AC3 and AC5 is minor rather than major because AC5 adds breadth (all three scenarios), but the plan does not explain what AC5 adds beyond AC3 for the mixed-status scenario specifically.
findings.suggested_edit: Merge AC3 into AC5, or clarify in AC5 that it is the aggregate summary and AC3 is the specific regression test flip.

findings.location: N/A
Title vs body; AC1–AC5
findings.finding: The title promises 'legend-or-option-domain anchoring + verbose-option value-synonyms (mixed-status on real snap)'. The body diagnoses exactly those two causes (legend too generic → AC1; verbose options causing polarity ties → AC2) and targets the mixed-status resolution on real data (AC3). AC4 preserves existing behaviour (regression guard). There is no scope drift — the plan neither adds unrelated features nor silently omits a titled deliverable. The change in pickBest/groupCandidates behaviour is implicitly acknowledged via the regression AC ('46 scenario-match checks … stay green'), which covers callers of the old behaviour.

findings.location: Plan body: two compounding causes (legend anchoring + verbose option value-synonyms); AC list (5 items)
AC3: 'the A-4c documented RED test is flipped to GREEN'; AC2: '4 abstract immigration values bind to the 4 real verbose option labels distinctly'
findings.finding: The plan introduces two distinct new logic paths (legend/option-domain anchoring and value-synonym/polarity tie-breaking for verbose option labels), yet the only observable test specification is 'flip one RED to GREEN'. There is no articulated failure-path coverage: what happens when the legend is absent entirely, when option-domain coverage is partial, or when the verbose option set grows beyond 4 entries? The plan does not describe test cases for the negative/boundary paths of the new anchoring heuristic (e.g. a group whose options happen to share enough tokens to look like the citizenship domain but are not), nor for the polarity rule when a newly added option shares the same head token. Without these, the new logic paths are covered only by a single known-good (happy-path) scenario, satisfying AC3 but leaving the new branching logic brittle. This is not a one-criterion triviality — it is new matching logic with multiple code paths.
findings.suggested_edit: Add to the acceptance criteria: (a) a negative test where a group's options do NOT cover the citizenship value domain and the control is correctly NOT selected; (b) a test where the legend is absent but option-domain anchoring alone resolves correctly; (c) a test where 3 of 4 verbose option labels share 'citizen' and the polarity rule still disambiguates; (d) specify an observable assertion for each (e.g. 'resolves to controlId X' or 'returns NO_CANDIDATE'), not just 'stays green'.

findings.location: Plan body: describes pure in-process text/token matching on captured snapshot data
Plan body: pure in-process matcher logic, no external calls, no writes, no stateful transitions
Plan body: no new endpoints, no data storage, no PII transmission, no auth boundaries introduced
AC1: 'anchoring on the legend OR on option-domain coverage'; AC2: 'polarity rule that ignores a shared head token'; References: 'ADR-0039 (A-4c)'
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new option-domain coverage heuristic and an updated polarity rule — both are novel matching rules that other scenarios (ESAP, destitute-migrant, future question types) could be affected by. The plan references ADR-0039 (A-4c) as the governing ADR but does not state whether the new anchoring strategy and the revised polarity rule will be captured as an ADR update or appended to ADR-0039. Without access to the codebase it is unclear whether ADR-0039 is being updated in-scope or whether a new ADR section is planned. If neither happens, future maintainers have no documented rationale for why option-domain coverage overrides the legend layer, or why the polarity rule now ignores shared head tokens. The changeability sub-check also raises a minor concern: the 'option-domain coverage' threshold (how many options must match to count as 'coverage'?) appears to be an implicit hardcoded value; the plan does not indicate it will be configurable.
findings.suggested_edit: Add an explicit AC or note: 'ADR-0039 is updated (or a new ADR-0040 is created) to document (a) the option-domain anchoring heuristic and its precedence over legend matching, and (b) the revised polarity rule rationale.' Also state the coverage threshold explicitly (e.g. 'all N abstract domain values must match at least one real option') so it is reviewable and, if needed, configurable.

findings.location: AC1 ('anchoring on the legend OR on option-domain coverage'), AC2 ('robust value-synonyms / a polarity rule that ignores a shared head token')
findings.finding: The plan introduces two novel heuristics—(1) option-domain coverage as a fallback group-anchor when the legend is too generic, and (2) a polarity rule that ignores a shared 'head token' across verbose option labels—neither of which is derived from data or a prior validated prototype. Both are asserted design choices: the plan says the legend 'carries no immigration/citizenship/legal token' and that polarity anti-tokens 'subtract equally,' but there is no spike or fixture-RED step that first measures whether the proposed domain-coverage threshold or the revised polarity rule actually resolves the ambiguity before the full implementation is built. The A-4c plan-review findings F1/F4b are cited as verification that the causes exist, but no empirical probe (e.g., a minimal script exercising the two new rules against a4c-real-snapshots.json) is included in this plan.
findings.suggested_edit: Add an explicit spike/probe step: before wiring into the engine, run the proposed option-domain-coverage scoring and the head-token-ignoring polarity rule against a4c-real-snapshots.json and confirm (a) the citizenship group is uniquely selected, and (b) all 4 verbose option labels bind distinctly with no tie. Record the threshold/rule values that produce this outcome and pin them as fixtures that must stay GREEN.
